rows.notes: Top-line title is centered and very clear; no monetary columns present. Lettering legible and unambiguous.

rows.description: a secundo die Novembris 1774 ad eundem
rows.notes: Second header line immediately under title. The year '1774' is clearly visible near 'Novembris'. No amounts present.
Third header line clear: 'diem revolutum 1775'. No monetary values.
Section heading centered under ruled line. Handwriting legible; no amounts on this line.
Amounts '30 0 0' are neatly aligned under the three right-hand columns (pounds/shillings/pence). Vertical guides clear; digits unambiguous.

rows.description: South Newington Rect: pro dim: An.d Mic:
rows.notes: Line reads '30 0 0' clearly. Description handwriting slightly flourished but unambiguous in context of parish name; numeric columns well aligned.
Pounds and shillings '88 5' are clear. Pence appears as '1¾' (three‑quarters of a penny) — the small '¾' sign is present but slightly stylized; column alignment supports this reading.
Pounds and shillings '28 11' clear. Pence reads as '10¾' (ten and three‑quarters pence) — the fraction mark is present but slightly faint; columns well aligned.
Numbers read as '07 5 02' with pence '02' (2d). Alignment with vertical rules and the identical pattern on the following similar row support reading of '2' pence.
Mirrors the previous row numerically: '07 5 02'. The pence digits align with the same column and match the shape on the Garsington line (clear '2').

rows.description: Tintinhull Napper pro trien: et dim:
rows.notes: Pounds/shillings '20 7' are clear. Pence appears as '9½' (nine and a half pence) — a small half‑penny loop present after the '9'. Fraction reading fairly clear though the loop is slightly light.
Amounts '02 3 6' are crisply written and well aligned in the pence column (6d). The parish name has a small abbreviation but the numbers are unambiguous.
Pounds and shillings '05 13' clear. Pence shows '6' with a small half‑penny loop (interpreted as 6½d). The halfpenny loop is somewhat faint but present; alignment supports reading.
Clear '03 0 0' in the three numeric columns. Description short and legible.

rows.description: Back Gate, Draper, pro An. & pfter parte
rows.notes: Numeric trio '11 5 0' clearly written and aligned. The description contains abbreviations ('An. & pfter parte') but is legible enough to transcribe as shown.
Pounds/shillings/pence read as '13 6 8'. The pence '8' is slightly crowded by nearby ink/shadow but aligns with the pence column and is clearly shaped.
Values '03 6 8' align under columns and match the style of the preceding row; small shadow at left does not affect numeric legibility.

rows.description: Sir Gregory Turner, 3 closes pro Anno
rows.notes: Pounds/shillings '16 7' clear. Pence reads as '10¾' (ten and three‑quarters pence) — the '¾' symbol is visible though slightly compressed; column alignment supports this reading.
Amounts read '01 18 0' clearly. The parenthetical note '(super Franklin)' is a best reading of the cursive; wording transcribed as appears though parenthetical text is somewhat compressed.
Signature or clerk's name at bottom right. Handwriting is stylized and partially obscured by a light stain/shadow; transcription 'Sayer olin Myner' is uncertain. No monetary columns on this line.
